name: Shield Dome
summary: A grounded Cesium half-dome with grid lines, scan bands, energy pulses, and an optional glowing base ring.
useCases: Protected zone or emergency command range
Energy shield, defense perimeter, or futuristic safety area

center.description: WGS84 dome center point. Optional height is supported.

color.description: CSS color used by the dome material.

gridDensity.description: Grid cell density in the dome material.

minimal.code:
import { createShieldDomeEffect } from '@ztgkzhaohao/geo-effect-kit'

const dome = createShieldDomeEffect(viewer, {
  center: { longitude: 116.391, latitude: 39.907 },
  radiusMeters: 12000,
  type: 'hex',
  color: '#57f7ff',
  speed: 1,
  opacity: 0.56,
  gridDensity: 14,
  pulseStrength: 0.72,
})

// Great as a GitHub homepage demo.
dome.flyTo()
dome.destroy()

update.code:
dome.update({
  type: 'matrix',
  color: '#b9ff4a',
  speed: 2,
  gridDensity: 20,
  pulseStrength: 0.9,
})

notes: Pass an existing Cesium Viewer; the SDK does not create or own the viewer.
The effect uses a Cesium EllipsoidGraphics half-dome plus GeoShieldDomeMaterial.
Changing center or radiusMeters rebuilds dome geometry; material style updates live.
Call destroy() during page cleanup to remove the data source and render loop.
